slots.name: heading
slots.copy: See the customer platform at work.

body: Breeze AI agents working a real pipeline live — prospecting, follow-up, and support handled for you.
Marketing, sales, and service running on one connected CRM.
A walkthrough tailored to your stack, not a generic product tour.

copy.note: Submit and we'll email you within one business day to schedule your 30-minute demo. No sales pressure — just a walkthrough tailored to your stack.

rationale: The brief is a lead-gen capture opener, so the hero IS the conversion surface — I chose the hero-form-split genre archetype: a persuasion column (kicker, one display claim, a 'what you'll see in 30 minutes' proof block, and a 299,000+ customer stat line) beside the form panel that acts as the counterweight (first name, work email, company size, one clear 'Book my demo' submit) with a note that says exactly what happens next, honoring the 'never gate behind vague promises' instruction. No project seed models a form hero, so this is novel within the drawable split archetype. Surface is accent-wash — a warm gradient art band not yet used in the run, keeping the light-canvas invariant (never dark mid-page) while licensing the form panel to nest. Clean-SaaS discipline: on-grid split, even alignment context, single accent reserved to the submit action, sentence-case heading at display tier, no off-grid treatments.
